subject: ap-chemistry
title: Kinetics: 1,3-Butadiene Dimerization
stimulus:
At high temperatures the compound C$_4$H$_6$ (1,3-butadiene) dimerizes: 2 C$_4$H$_6$(g) → C$_8$H$_{12}$(g). The rate of
the reaction was studied at 625 K in a rigid reaction vessel. Two different trials, each with a different starting
concentration, were carried out. The data were plotted in three different ways, as shown below.

parts.prompt:
For trial 1, calculate the initial pressure, in atm, in the vessel at 625 K. Assume that initially all the gas
present in the vessel is C$_4$H$_6$.
parts.scoring_notes: From the graphs, the initial concentration in trial 1 is approximately [C$_4$H$_6$]$_0$ = 0.020 mol/L. Use ideal gas law: P = nRT/V = cRT. P = (0.020 mol/L)(0.08206 L·atm/mol·K)(625 K) = 1.026 ≈ 1.03 atm.

scoring_points.description: For correct pressure using P = cRT with the initial concentration from the graphs: 1 point

alternatives.correct_example:
From the graph, initial [C$_4$H$_6$] in trial 1 = 0.020 M.
$P = [C_4H_6]RT = (0.020\text{ mol/L})(0.08206)(625) = 1.03\text{ atm}$

wrong_examples: Using T in °C — must use T = 625 K in the gas law.
Dividing RT instead of multiplying — $P = cRT$, not $c/(RT)$.
common_traps: Pressure from concentration: $P = n/V \times RT = c \times RT$. For a gas at concentration $c$ (mol/L), $P = cRT$.

parts.prompt: Use the data plotted in the graphs to determine the order of the reaction with respect to C$_4$H$_6$.
parts.scoring_notes: Second order. The graph of 1/[C$_4$H$_6$] versus time gives a straight line for both trials (both trials superimpose on the same line in the 1/[C$_4$H$_6$] graph), which is the diagnostic for second-order kinetics. The [A] vs. t and ln[A] vs. t plots are curved.

scoring_points.description: For 'second order' with correct identification of the linear 1/[A] vs. t graph: 1 point

required_elements: The graph of 1/[C$_4$H$_6$] vs. time is linear — this is diagnostic for a second-order reaction.
alternatives.correct_example: Second order. The integrated rate law for a second-order reaction is $1/[A] = kt + 1/[A]_0$, which gives a straight line on a 1/[A] vs. t plot. The data show the 1/[C$_4$H$_6$] plot is linear while the [C$_4$H$_6$] and ln[C$_4$H$_6$] plots are curved.

wrong_examples: First order — first order gives a linear ln[A] vs. t plot, not a linear 1/[A] vs. t plot.
Zero order — zero order gives a linear [A] vs. t plot.
common_traps: Diagnostic linearization: zero order → [A] vs. t; first order → ln[A] vs. t; second order → 1/[A] vs. t.

parts.prompt:
The initial rate of the reaction in trial 1 is 0.0010 mol/(L·s). Calculate the rate constant, $k$, for the reaction
at 625 K.

scoring_points.description: For the correct $k$ value with units L/(mol·s): 1 point
wrong_examples: Reporting k without units — units of $k$ for a second-order reaction are M$^{-1}$s$^{-1}$ = L/(mol·s).
Using first-order formula k = rate/[A] — for second order, divide by [A]$^2$.
